paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jacob dwelt in the land of his father`s sojournings, in the land of Canaan.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: These are the generations of Jacob. Joseph, being seventeen years old, was feeding the flock with his brethren; and he was a lad with the sons of Bilhah, and with the sons of Zilpah, his father`s wives: and Joseph brought the evil report of them unto their father.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Now Israel loved Joseph more than all his children, because he was the son of his old age: and he made him a coat of many colors.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And his brethren saw that their father loved him more than all his brethren; and they hated him, and could not speak peaceably unto him.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Joseph dreamed a dream, and he told it to his brethren: and they hated him yet the more.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And he said unto them, Hear, I pray you, this dream which I have dreamed:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: for, behold, we were binding sheaves in the field, and, lo, my sheaf arose, and also stood upright; and, behold, your sheaves came round about, and made obeisance to my sheaf.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And his brethren said to him, Shalt thou indeed reign over us? Or shalt thou indeed have dominion over us? And they hated him yet the more for his dreams, and for his words.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And he dreamed yet another dream, and told it to his brethren, and said, Behold, I have dreamed yet a dream: and, behold, the sun and the moon and eleven stars made obeisance to me.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And he told it to his father, and to his brethren; and his father rebuked him, and said unto him, What is this dream that thou hast dreamed? Shall I and thy mother and thy brethren indeed come to bow down ourselves to thee to the earth?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And his brethren envied him; but his father kept the saying in mind.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And his brethren went to feed their father`s flock in Shechem.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Israel said unto Joseph, Are not thy brethren feeding the flock in Shechem? Come, and I will send thee unto them. And he said to him, Here am I.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And he said to him, Go now, see whether it is well with thy brethren, and well with the flock; and bring me word again. So he sent him out of the vale of Hebron, and he came to Shechem.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And a certain man found him, and, behold, he was wandering in the field: and the man asked him, saying, What seekest thou?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And he said, I am seeking my brethren: tell me, I pray thee, where they are feeding [the flock].

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the man said, They are departed hence; for I heard them say, Let us go to Dothan. And Joseph went after his brethren, and found them in Dothan.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And they saw him afar off, and before he came near unto them, they conspired against him to slay him.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And they said one to another, Behold, this dreamer cometh.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Come now therefore, and let us slay him, and cast him into one of the pits, and we will say, And evil beast hath devoured him: and we shall see what will become of his dreams.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Reuben heard it, and delivered him out of their hand, and said, Let us not take his life.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Reuben said unto them, Shed no blood; cast him into this pit that is in the wilderness, but lay no hand upon him: that he might deliver him out of their hand, to restore him to his father.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And it came to pass, when Joseph was come unto his brethren, that they stripped Joseph of his coat, the coat of many colors that was on him;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and they took him, and cast him into the pit: and the pit was empty, there was no water in it.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And they sat down to eat bread: and they lifted up their eyes and looked, and, behold, a caravan of Ishmaelites was coming from Gilead, with their camels bearing spicery and balm and myrrh, going to carry it down to Egypt.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Judah said unto his brethren, What profit is it if we slay our brother and conceal his blood?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Come, and let us sell him to the Ishmaelites, and let not our hand be upon him; for he is our brother, our flesh. And his brethren hearkened unto him.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And there passed by Midianites, merchantmen; and they drew and lifted up Joseph out of the pit, and sold Joseph to the Ishmaelites for twenty pieces of silver. And they brought Joseph into Egypt.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Reuben returned unto the pit; and, behold, Joseph was not in the pit; and he rent his clothes.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And he returned unto his brethren, and said, The child is not; and I, whither shall I go?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And they took Joseph`s coat, and killed a he-goat, and dipped the coat in the blood;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and they sent the coat of many colors, and they brought it to their father, and said, This have we found: know now whether it is thy son`s coat or not.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And he knew it, and said, It is my son`s coat: an evil beast hath devoured him; Joseph is without doubt torn in pieces.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jacob rent his garments, and put sackcloth upon his loins, and mourned for his son many days.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And all his sons and all his daughters rose up to comfort him; but he refused to be comforted; and he said, For I will go down to Sheol to my son mourning. And his father wept for him.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the Midianites sold him into Egypt unto Potiphar, an officer of Pharaoh`s, the captain of the guard.
